reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification): Code reading is accurate. The handle_blobs_by_range_request_inner function at rpc_methods.rs:1063-1079 serves all blobs from chain.get_blobs() for slots in the requested range, with no fork-epoch filtering. Only slot-range validation exists (lines 1069-1070). Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): Confirmed spec deviation from PROP-6a4369e9-inv-029 (01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json, lines 787-803): 'During transition, blob_sidecars_by_range must serve only pre-Fulu epoch blobs. Assertion: forall sidecar in transition_resp: compute_epoch(sidecar.slot) < FULU_FORK_EPOCH'. The code lacks this epoch boundary check. Exploitability: Uncertain. Post-Fulu blocks likely store only DataColumnSidecars (not BlobSidecars) per PeerDAS architecture, so get_blobs() may naturally return empty for post-Fulu slots. However, the spec requirement exists to prevent protocol confusion during fork transition—compliant clients would reject post-Fulu blob requests, while this implementation would attempt to serve them (even if empty). This is a clear spec non-compliance for a Fusaka-specific fork transition requirement. Severity: Medium maintained per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json—affects protocol correctness during fork transition (>5% of network during transition window), single RPC vector, but actual exploitation requires post-Fulu blob sidecars to exist in storage.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: During transition, blob_sidecars_by_range must serve only pre-Fulu epoch blobs. Assertion: forall sidecar in transition_resp: compute_epoch(sidecar.slot) < FULU_FORK_EPOCH (PROP-6a4369e9-inv-029, 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json)
